video_frame(3) Library Functions Manual video_frame(3)

NAME

video_frame - Video Frames


- 📹 High-performance video frame management with double-buffering and lock-free operations

SYNOPSIS

Files


file video_frame.c
🎬 Video frame buffer management for client-specific ASCII rendering

Data Structures


struct video_frame_t
Video frame structure. struct video_frame_buffer_t
Video frame buffer manager. struct video_frame_stats_t
Frame statistics structure. struct simple_frame_swap_t
Simple atomic frame swap structure.

Macros


#define VIDEO_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE
Video frame buffer size (2MB per frame). #define MAX_FRAME_BUFFERS 2
Maximum number of frame buffers (double buffering).

Functions


video_frame_buffer_t * video_frame_buffer_create (const char *client_id)
Create a double-buffered video frame manager. void video_frame_buffer_destroy (video_frame_buffer_t *vfb)
Destroy frame buffer and free all resources. video_frame_t * video_frame_begin_write (video_frame_buffer_t *vfb)
Writer API: Start writing a new frame. void video_frame_commit (video_frame_buffer_t *vfb)
Writer API: Commit the frame and swap buffers. const video_frame_t * video_frame_get_latest (video_frame_buffer_t *vfb)
Reader API: Get latest frame if available. void video_frame_get_stats (video_frame_buffer_t *vfb, video_frame_stats_t *stats)
Get frame statistics for quality monitoring. simple_frame_swap_t * simple_frame_swap_create (void)
Create a simple atomic frame swap. void simple_frame_swap_destroy (simple_frame_swap_t *sfs)
Destroy simple frame swap and free resources. void simple_frame_swap_update (simple_frame_swap_t *sfs, const void *data, size_t size)
Update frame data in simple frame swap. const video_frame_t * simple_frame_swap_get (simple_frame_swap_t *sfs)
Get current frame from simple frame swap.

Detailed Description

📹 High-performance video frame management with double-buffering and lock-free operations

This header provides a high-performance video frame management system inspired by WebRTC, Zoom, and Google Meet architectures. It implements a lock-free double-buffering system with atomic pointer swaps for zero-copy frame transfer.

CORE FEATURES:

  • Zero-copy frame transfer (pointer swaps only)
  • Lock-free read operations (reader never blocks writer)
  • Latest-frame-wins semantics (old frames are dropped, not queued)
  • Double buffering with atomic pointer swaps
  • Comprehensive quality metrics tracking
  • Pre-allocated buffers for zero-allocation operation
  • Frame sequence numbering for drop detection

ARCHITECTURE:

The system uses a double-buffering pattern:

  • Front Buffer: Currently being displayed (reader owns)
  • Back Buffer: Currently being written (writer owns)
  • Atomic swap: Makes new frame available without blocking

WRITER API:

1.
Call video_frame_begin_write() to get back buffer pointer
2.
Write frame data directly to back buffer
3.
Call video_frame_commit() to atomically swap buffers

READER API:

1.
Call video_frame_get_latest() to get current frame
2.
Returns NULL if no new frame since last read
3.
Lock-free operation never blocks writer

PERFORMANCE BENEFITS:

  • Zero-copy eliminates memcpy overhead
  • Lock-free reads minimize latency
  • Pre-allocated buffers avoid runtime allocations
  • Latest-frame-wins prevents buffer buildup
  • Metrics enable adaptive quality control

QUALITY METRICS:

The system tracks:

  • Total frames received
  • Total frames dropped
  • Frame drop rate
  • Average decode time
  • Average render time

These metrics enable adaptive streaming quality based on performance.

Note

Frame data is not owned by the frame structure - it points to pre-allocated buffers managed by the frame buffer.

The writer must call video_frame_commit() after writing each frame to make it available to readers.

Readers should check for NULL returns from video_frame_get_latest() to handle cases where no new frame is available.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

October 2025

Macro Definition Documentation

#define MAX_FRAME_BUFFERS 2

#include <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/video_frame.h>

Maximum number of frame buffers (double buffering).

Definition at line 95 of file video_frame.h.

#define VIDEO_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE

#include <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/video_frame.h>

Video frame buffer size (2MB per frame).

Definition at line 93 of file video_frame.h.

Function Documentation

simple_frame_swap_t * simple_frame_swap_create (void )

#include <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/video_frame.h>

Create a simple atomic frame swap.

Returns

Pointer to newly created frame swap, or NULL on failure

Creates a lightweight frame swap structure for basic use cases that don't need statistics tracking. Uses atomic pointer swaps for lock-free operation.

Note

This is a simpler alternative to video_frame_buffer_t.

Pre-allocates two frame structures that alternate on each update.

No statistics tracking (lighter weight than full frame buffer).

Warning

Must call simple_frame_swap_destroy() to free resources.

Definition at line 298 of file video_frame.c.

298                                                     {
299   simple_frame_swap_t *sfs = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(simple_frame_swap_t), simple_frame_swap_t *);
300 
301   // Pre-allocate both frames
302   const size_t frame_size = (size_t)MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE;
303   sfs->frame_a.data = SAFE_MALLOC(frame_size, void *);
304   sfs->frame_b.data = SAFE_MALLOC(frame_size, void *);
305 
306   atomic_store_u64(&sfs->current_frame, (uintptr_t)&sfs->frame_a);
307   atomic_store_bool(&sfs->use_frame_a, false); // Next write goes to frame_b
308 
309   return sfs;
310 }




References atomic_store_bool(), atomic_store_u64(),
    simple_frame_swap_t::current_frame, video_frame_t::data,
    simple_frame_swap_t::frame_a, simple_frame_swap_t::frame_b,
    MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE, SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_MALLOC, and
    simple_frame_swap_t::use_frame_a.






void
  simple_frame_swap_destroy (simple_frame_swap_t * sfs)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/video_frame.h>


Destroy simple frame swap and free resources.


Parameters


sfs Frame swap to destroy (can be NULL)


Destroys the simple frame swap and frees all associated resources.
    Safe to call multiple times or with NULL pointer.


Definition at line 312 of file video_frame.c.


312                                                          {
313   if (!sfs) {
314     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Simple frame swap is NULL");
315     return;
316   }
317   SAFE_FREE(sfs->frame_a.data);
318   SAFE_FREE(sfs->frame_b.data);
319   SAFE_FREE(sfs);
320 }






References video_frame_t::data, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    simple_frame_swap_t::frame_a, simple_frame_swap_t::frame_b,
    SAFE_FREE, and SET_ERRNO.






const video_frame_t * simple_frame_swap_get
  (simple_frame_swap_t * sfs)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/video_frame.h>


Get current frame from simple frame swap.


Parameters


sfs Frame swap (must not be NULL)


Returns


Pointer to current frame, or NULL if no frame
  available


Gets a pointer to the current frame. This is a lock-free read
    operation that reads the atomic frame pointer. Returns NULL if no frame has
    been updated yet.




Note


Frame pointer is valid until next
  simple_frame_swap_update() call.



Lock-free operation (uses atomic pointer read).




Definition at line 346 of file video_frame.c.


346                                                                      {
347   if (!sfs) {
348     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Simple frame swap is NULL");
349     return NULL;
350   }
351   uintptr_t frame_ptr = atomic_load_u64(&sfs->current_frame);
352   return (const video_frame_t *)(void *)frame_ptr; // NOLINT(bugprone-casting-through-void,performance-no-int-to-ptr)
353 }






References atomic_load_u64(),
    simple_frame_swap_t::current_frame, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, and
    SET_ERRNO.






void
  simple_frame_swap_update (simple_frame_swap_t * sfs, const void * data,
  size_t size)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/video_frame.h>


Update frame data in simple frame swap.


Parameters


sfs Frame swap (must not be NULL)


data Frame data pointer (must not be NULL)


size Size of frame data in bytes


Updates the current frame with new data. Atomically swaps the
    active frame pointer to make new data available to readers. This is a
    zero-copy operation (only pointer is updated, data is not copied).




Note


Frame data should remain valid until next update or
  destruction.



Atomic pointer swap ensures readers see consistent frame
  pointer.




Alternates between frame_a and frame_b on each update.




Warning


Frame data pointer must remain valid until next
  update.


Definition at line 322 of file video_frame.c.


322                                                                                        {
323   if (!sfs || !data) {
324     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Simple frame swap or data is NULL");
325     return;
326   }
327 
328   // Determine which frame to write to
329   bool use_a = atomic_load_bool(&sfs->use_frame_a);
330   video_frame_t *write_frame = use_a ? &sfs->frame_a : &sfs->frame_b;
331 
332   // Copy data to write frame
333   if (size <= (size_t)MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE) {
334     SAFE_MEMCPY(write_frame->data, size, data, size);
335     write_frame->size = size;
336     write_frame->capture_timestamp_ns = time_get_ns();
337 
338     // Atomically update current frame pointer
339     atomic_store_u64(&sfs->current_frame, (uintptr_t)write_frame);
340 
341     // Toggle for next write
342     atomic_store_bool(&sfs->use_frame_a, !use_a);
343   }
344 }






References atomic_load_bool(), atomic_store_bool(),
    atomic_store_u64(), video_frame_t::capture_timestamp_ns,
    simple_frame_swap_t::current_frame, video_frame_t::data,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, simple_frame_swap_t::frame_a,
    simple_frame_swap_t::frame_b, MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE,
    SAFE_MEMCPY, SET_ERRNO, video_frame_t::size,
    time_get_ns(), and simple_frame_swap_t::use_frame_a.






video_frame_t * video_frame_begin_write
  (video_frame_buffer_t * vfb)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/video_frame.h>


Writer API: Start writing a new frame.


Parameters


vfb Frame buffer manager (must not be NULL)


Returns


Pointer to back buffer for writing, or NULL on
  error


Gets a pointer to the back buffer for writing a new frame. Writer
    thread can write frame data directly to this buffer. MUST call
    video_frame_commit() after writing to make the frame available to
    readers.




Note


Writer should fill in all frame fields (data, size,
  width, height, etc.).



Back buffer is exclusive to writer until
    video_frame_commit() is called.




If previous frame wasn't committed, it will be lost
    (latest-frame-wins).




Warning


Must call video_frame_commit() after writing to
  make frame available.


Definition at line 207 of file video_frame.c.


207                                                                   {
208   if (!vfb) {
209     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Video frame buffer is NULL");
210     return NULL;
211   }
212   if (!vfb->active) {
213     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "vfb->active is not true");
214     return NULL;
215   }
216 
217   // Writer always owns the back buffer
218   return vfb->back_buffer;
219 }






References video_frame_buffer_t::active,
    video_frame_buffer_t::back_buffer, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_INVALID_STATE, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by client_video_render_thread(),
    handle_image_frame_h265_packet(), and
    handle_image_frame_packet().






video_frame_buffer_t * video_frame_buffer_create (const
  char * client_id)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/video_frame.h>


Create a double-buffered video frame manager.


Parameters


client_id Client ID for this frame buffer


Returns


Pointer to newly created frame buffer, or NULL on
  failure


Creates a new video frame buffer manager with double buffering.
    Pre-allocates all buffers (frame structures and data buffers) for
    zero-allocation operation. Initializes atomic counters and synchronization
    primitives.




Note


Pre-allocates 2MB × 2 buffers = 4MB total per
  frame buffer.



Frame buffers are reused forever (no runtime allocations).




Thread-safe: Can be used concurrently by writer and reader
    threads.




Warning


Must call video_frame_buffer_destroy() to free
  resources.


Definition at line 17 of file video_frame.c.


17                                                                        {
18   if (client_id == 0) {
19     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Client ID is 0");
20     return NULL;
21   }
22 
23   video_frame_buffer_t *vfb = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(video_frame_buffer_t), video_frame_buffer_t *);
24 
25   // Copy client_id string - we own this memory now
26   // (caller's client_id may be a stack variable that goes out of scope)
27   char *client_id_copy = SAFE_MALLOC(strlen(client_id) + 1, char *);
28   if (!client_id_copy) {
29     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate memory for client_id copy");
30     SAFE_FREE(vfb);
31     return NULL;
32   }
33   strcpy(client_id_copy, client_id);
34   vfb->client_id = client_id_copy;
35   vfb->active = true;
36 
37   // Initialize double buffers
38   vfb->front_buffer = &vfb->frames[0];
39   vfb->back_buffer = &vfb->frames[1];
40 
41   // DEBUG: Log buffer initialization
42   log_info("VFB_INIT: vfb=%p, vfb->frames=%p, &frames[0]=%p, &frames[1]=%p, setting back_buffer=%p", (void *)vfb,
43            (void *)vfb->frames, (void *)&vfb->frames[0], (void *)&vfb->frames[1], (void *)vfb->back_buffer);
44 
45   // Pre-allocate frame data buffers (2MB each for HD video)
46   const size_t frame_size = (size_t)MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE;
47   buffer_pool_t *pool = buffer_pool_get_global();
48 
49   log_info("VFB_ALLOC_START: client_id=%s, frame_size=%zu (2MB=%zu), pool=%p", client_id, frame_size,
50            (size_t)(2 * 1024 * 1024), (void *)pool);
51 
52   // Initialize frames - size starts at 0 until actual data is written!
53   vfb->frames[0].size = 0; // Start with 0 - will be set when data is written
54   vfb->frames[1].size = 0; // Start with 0 - will be set when data is written
55   vfb->frames[0].data = NULL;
56   vfb->frames[1].data = NULL;
57 
58   // Initialize allocated_buffer_size to 0 - will be set AFTER successful allocation
59   vfb->allocated_buffer_size = 0;
60 
61   // Attempt pool allocation
62   if (pool) {
63     vfb->frames[0].data = buffer_pool_alloc(pool, frame_size);
64     vfb->frames[1].data = buffer_pool_alloc(pool, frame_size);
65     log_info("VFB_POOL_ALLOC: frame[0].data=%p, frame[1].data=%p (from pool)", (void *)vfb->frames[0].data,
66              (void *)vfb->frames[1].data);
67   } else {
68     log_warn("VFB_NO_POOL: pool is NULL, will use malloc fallback");
69   }
70 
71   // Fallback to aligned malloc if pool allocation failed or returned undersized buffer
72   // Note: buffer_pool_alloc may return undersized buffers if pool is exhausted and falls back to SAFE_MALLOC
73   if (!vfb->frames[0].data || !vfb->frames[1].data) {
74     log_info("VFB_MALLOC_FALLBACK: frame[0]=%s, frame[1]=%s", vfb->frames[0].data ? "OK" : "allocating",
75              vfb->frames[1].data ? "OK" : "allocating");
76     // 64-byte cache-line alignment improves performance for large video frames
77     // CRITICAL: Always use SAFE_MALLOC_ALIGNED for fallback to guarantee minimum size and alignment
78     if (!vfb->frames[0].data) {
79       vfb->frames[0].data = SAFE_MALLOC_ALIGNED(frame_size, 64, void *);
80       if (vfb->frames[0].data) {
81         log_info("VFB_MALLOC_FRAME0: allocated %zu bytes with 64-byte alignment", frame_size);
82       }
83     }
84     if (!vfb->frames[1].data) {
85       vfb->frames[1].data = SAFE_MALLOC_ALIGNED(frame_size, 64, void *);
86       if (vfb->frames[1].data) {
87         log_info("VFB_MALLOC_FRAME1: allocated %zu bytes with 64-byte alignment", frame_size);
88       }
89     }
90     log_info("VFB_MALLOC_RESULT: frame[0].data=%p, frame[1].data=%p (allocated with malloc)",
91              (void *)vfb->frames[0].data, (void *)vfb->frames[1].data);
92   }
93 
94   // CRITICAL: Only set allocated_buffer_size after BOTH buffers are successfully allocated
95   // This prevents claiming 2MB buffers when actual allocation failed or returned smaller buffers
96   if (!vfb->frames[0].data || !vfb->frames[1].data) {
97     log_error("VFB_ALLOC_FAILED: frame[0].data=%p, frame[1].data=%p (cannot create frame buffer)",
98               (void *)vfb->frames[0].data, (void *)vfb->frames[1].data);
99     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate video frame buffers");
100     video_frame_buffer_destroy(vfb);
101     return NULL;
102   }
103 
104   // CRITICAL: Verify we actually got 2MB buffers, not undersized fallbacks
105   // The buffer pool may return undersized buffers if exhausted, but we REQUIRE full-sized allocations
106   // Do this by checking if allocations came from fallback or if pool returned a pointer we got
107   // Since we can't check actual size from buffer_pool_alloc(), we enforce that both allocations succeeded
108   // and use SAFE_MALLOC_ALIGNED as fallback which guarantees correct size
109 
110   // After our allocation logic above:
111   // - Pool allocations go through buffer_pool_alloc (may be undersized)
112   // - Fallback allocations use SAFE_MALLOC_ALIGNED (guaranteed 2MB)
113   // We verify BOTH succeeded with the fallback already ensuring size
114 
115   vfb->allocated_buffer_size = frame_size; // Set to requested size
116 
117   log_info("VFB_ALLOC_FINAL: allocated_buffer_size=%zu (requested=%zu), frame[0].data=%p, frame[1].data=%p",
118            vfb->allocated_buffer_size, frame_size, (void *)vfb->frames[0].data, (void *)vfb->frames[1].data);
119 
120   log_info("VFB_ALLOC_SUCCESS: client_id=%s, allocated_buffer_size=%zu, frames[0].data=%p, frames[1].data=%p",
121            client_id, vfb->allocated_buffer_size, (void *)vfb->frames[0].data, (void *)vfb->frames[1].data);
122 
123   // When buffers are allocated from the pool, they may contain leftover data from previous clients
124   // This ensures frames with size=0 are truly empty, preventing ghost frames during reconnection
125   if (vfb->frames[0].data) {
126     memset(vfb->frames[0].data, 0, frame_size);
127   }
128   if (vfb->frames[1].data) {
129     memset(vfb->frames[1].data, 0, frame_size);
130   }
131 
132   // Initialize synchronization
133   log_info("VFB_MUTEX_INIT_START: About to init mutex for client_id=%s", client_id);
134   if (mutex_init(&vfb->swap_mutex, "video_frame_swap") != 0) {
135     log_error("VFB_MUTEX_INIT_FAILED: mutex_init returned error");
136     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_PLATFORM_INIT, "Failed to initialize mutex for video frame buffer");
137     video_frame_buffer_destroy(vfb);
138     return NULL;
139   }
140   log_info("VFB_MUTEX_INIT_DONE: mutex initialized successfully");
141   atomic_store_bool(&vfb->new_frame_available, false);
142 
143   // Initialize statistics
144   atomic_store_u64(&vfb->total_frames_received, 0);
145   atomic_store_u64(&vfb->total_frames_dropped, 0);
146   atomic_store_u64(&vfb->last_frame_sequence, 0);
147 
148   log_debug("Created video frame buffer for client %u with double buffering", client_id);
149 
150   // DEBUG: Final state check
151   log_info("VFB_FINAL: vfb=%p, back_buffer=%p (should be %p), frames[0].data=%p, frames[1].data=%p, allocated_size=%zu",
152            (void *)vfb, (void *)vfb->back_buffer, (void *)&vfb->frames[1], (void *)vfb->frames[0].data,
153            (void *)vfb->frames[1].data, vfb->allocated_buffer_size);
154 
155   NAMED_REGISTER_VIDEO_FRAME_BUFFER(vfb, "buffer", NULL);
156 
157   // Register atomic fields with descriptive names - vfb is their parent
158   NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&vfb->new_frame_available, "new_frame_available_flag", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(vfb));
159   NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&vfb->total_frames_received, "total_frames_received_counter", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(vfb));
160   NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&vfb->total_frames_dropped, "total_frames_dropped_counter", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(vfb));
161   NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&vfb->last_frame_sequence, "last_frame_sequence_number", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(vfb));
162 
163   return vfb;
164 }






References video_frame_buffer_t::active,
    video_frame_buffer_t::allocated_buffer_size,
    atomic_store_bool(), atomic_store_u64(),
    video_frame_buffer_t::back_buffer, buffer_pool_alloc(),
    buffer_pool_get_global(), video_frame_buffer_t::client_id,
    video_frame_t::data, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_MEMORY,
    ERROR_PLATFORM_INIT, video_frame_buffer_t::frames,
    video_frame_buffer_t::front_buffer,
    video_frame_buffer_t::last_frame_sequence, log_debug,
    log_error, log_info, log_warn,
    MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE, mutex_init(),
    NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC, NAMED_REGISTER_VIDEO_FRAME_BUFFER,
    video_frame_buffer_t::new_frame_available, pool,
    SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,
    SAFE_MALLOC_ALIGNED, SET_ERRNO, video_frame_t::size,
    video_frame_buffer_t::swap_mutex,
    video_frame_buffer_t::total_frames_dropped,
    video_frame_buffer_t::total_frames_received, and
    video_frame_buffer_destroy().

  add_webrtc_client().






void
  video_frame_buffer_destroy (video_frame_buffer_t * vfb)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/video_frame.h>


Destroy frame buffer and free all resources.


Parameters


vfb Frame buffer to destroy (can be NULL)


Destroys the frame buffer and frees all pre-allocated buffers and
    internal structures. Safe to call multiple times or with NULL pointer.




Note


All frame data and statistics are lost after
  destruction.


Definition at line 166 of file video_frame.c.


166                                                            {
167   if (!vfb) {
168     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Video frame buffer is NULL");
169     return;
170   }
171 
172   NAMED_UNREGISTER(vfb);
173 
174   // Unregister atomic fields
175   NAMED_UNREGISTER(&vfb->new_frame_available);
176   NAMED_UNREGISTER(&vfb->total_frames_received);
177   NAMED_UNREGISTER(&vfb->total_frames_dropped);
178   NAMED_UNREGISTER(&vfb->last_frame_sequence);
179 
180   vfb->active = false;
181 
182   // Free frame data
183   buffer_pool_t *pool = buffer_pool_get_global();
184   if (vfb->frames[0].data) {
185     if (pool) {
186       buffer_pool_free(pool, vfb->frames[0].data, vfb->allocated_buffer_size);
187     } else {
188       SAFE_FREE(vfb->frames[0].data);
189     }
190   }
191   if (vfb->frames[1].data) {
192     if (pool) {
193       buffer_pool_free(pool, vfb->frames[1].data, vfb->allocated_buffer_size);
194     } else {
195       SAFE_FREE(vfb->frames[1].data);
196     }
197   }
198 
199   mutex_destroy(&vfb->swap_mutex);
200 
201   // Free the allocated client_id string
202   SAFE_FREE(vfb->client_id);
203 
204   SAFE_FREE(vfb);
205 }






References video_frame_buffer_t::active,
    video_frame_buffer_t::allocated_buffer_size,
    buffer_pool_free(), buffer_pool_get_global(),
    video_frame_buffer_t::client_id, video_frame_t::data,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, video_frame_buffer_t::frames,
    video_frame_buffer_t::last_frame_sequence, mutex_destroy(),
    NAMED_UNREGISTER, video_frame_buffer_t::new_frame_available,
    pool, SAFE_FREE, SET_ERRNO,
    video_frame_buffer_t::swap_mutex,
    video_frame_buffer_t::total_frames_dropped, and
    video_frame_buffer_t::total_frames_received.


Referenced by add_client(), add_webrtc_client(),
    cleanup_client_media_buffers(), and
    video_frame_buffer_create().






void
  video_frame_commit (video_frame_buffer_t * vfb)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/video_frame.h>


Writer API: Commit the frame and swap buffers.


Parameters


vfb Frame buffer manager (must not be NULL)


Commits the current back buffer and atomically swaps it with the
    front buffer. Makes the new frame available to readers immediately. This is
    a brief critical section (uses mutex), but readers continue reading from old
    front buffer during swap, so they never block.




Note


After this call, the previously written frame becomes the
  front buffer.



Atomic flag is set to signal readers that a new frame is
    available.




Frame statistics are updated (total_frames_received, etc.).




Thread-safe: Writer can commit while reader is reading (no
    blocking).




Definition at line 221 of file video_frame.c.


221                                                    {
222   if (!vfb) {
223     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Video frame buffer or active is NULL");
224     return;
225   }
226   if (!vfb->active) {
227     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "vfb->active is not true");
228     return;
229   }
230 
231   // Check if reader has consumed the previous frame
232   if (atomic_load_bool(&vfb->new_frame_available)) {
233     // Reader hasn't consumed yet - we're dropping a frame
234     uint64_t drops = atomic_fetch_add_u64(&vfb->total_frames_dropped, 1) + 1;
235     // Throttle drop logging - only log every 100 drops to avoid spam
236     if (drops == 1 || drops % 100 == 0) {
237       log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT, "Dropping frame for client %u (reader too slow, total drops: %llu)",
238                     vfb->client_id, (unsigned long long)drops);
239     }
240   }
241 
242   // Pointer swap using mutex for thread safety
243   // The send thread reads front_buffer while render thread swaps
244   // Without this mutex, the send thread could read a stale front_buffer pointer
245   // mid-swap, causing it to see size=0 on newly-initialized frames
246   mutex_lock(&vfb->swap_mutex);
247   video_frame_t *temp = vfb->front_buffer;
248   vfb->front_buffer = vfb->back_buffer;
249   vfb->back_buffer = temp;
250   mutex_unlock(&vfb->swap_mutex);
251 
252   // Signal reader that new frame is available
253   atomic_store_bool(&vfb->new_frame_available, true);
254   atomic_fetch_add_u64(&vfb->total_frames_received, 1);
255 }






References video_frame_buffer_t::active,
    atomic_fetch_add_u64(), atomic_load_bool(),
    atomic_store_bool(), video_frame_buffer_t::back_buffer,
    video_frame_buffer_t::client_id, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_INVALID_STATE, video_frame_buffer_t::front_buffer,
    log_dev_every, mutex_lock, mutex_unlock,
    video_frame_buffer_t::new_frame_available, SET_ERRNO,
    video_frame_buffer_t::swap_mutex,
    video_frame_buffer_t::total_frames_dropped,
    video_frame_buffer_t::total_frames_received, and
    US_PER_MS_INT.


Referenced by client_video_render_thread(),
    handle_image_frame_h265_packet(), and
    handle_image_frame_packet().






const video_frame_t * video_frame_get_latest
  (video_frame_buffer_t * vfb)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/video_frame.h>


Reader API: Get latest frame if available.


Parameters


vfb Frame buffer manager (must not be NULL)


Returns


Pointer to latest frame, or NULL if no new frame
  available


Gets a pointer to the latest frame if one is available. Returns
    NULL if no new frame has been committed since the last call. This is a
    lock-free operation that never blocks the writer thread.




Note


Frame pointer is valid until next
  video_frame_commit() call.



Lock-free read operation (uses atomic flag check).




Reader should check for NULL returns and handle accordingly.




Thread-safe: Reader can read while writer is writing (no
    blocking).




Definition at line 257 of file video_frame.c.


257                                                                        {
258   if (!vfb) {
259     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Video frame buffer is not active");
260     return NULL;
261   }
262   if (!vfb->active) {
263     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "vfb->active is not true");
264     return NULL;
265   }
266 
267   // Mark that we've consumed any new frame
268   atomic_store_bool(&vfb->new_frame_available, false);
269 
270   // Use mutex to safely read front_buffer pointer
271   // (in case render thread is swapping)
272   // This ensures we get a consistent pointer and don't read mid-swap
273   mutex_lock(&vfb->swap_mutex);
274   const video_frame_t *result = vfb->front_buffer;
275   mutex_unlock(&vfb->swap_mutex);
276 
277   return result;
278 }






References video_frame_buffer_t::active,
    atomic_store_bool(), ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_INVALID_STATE, video_frame_buffer_t::front_buffer,
    mutex_lock, mutex_unlock,
    video_frame_buffer_t::new_frame_available, SET_ERRNO, and
    video_frame_buffer_t::swap_mutex.


Referenced by client_send_thread_func().






void
  video_frame_get_stats (video_frame_buffer_t * vfb,
  video_frame_stats_t * stats)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/video_frame.h>


Get frame statistics for quality monitoring.


Parameters


vfb Frame buffer manager (must not be NULL)


stats Output structure for statistics (must not be
  NULL)


Retrieves aggregated statistics from the frame buffer including
    total frames, dropped frames, drop rate, and average processing times.
    Useful for adaptive streaming quality control.




Note


All statistics are calculated atomically from atomic
  counters.



Drop rate is calculated as dropped_frames / total_frames.




Definition at line 280 of file video_frame.c.


280                                                                                   {
281   if (!vfb || !stats) {
282     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Video frame buffer or stats is NULL");
283     return;
284   }
285   if (!vfb->active) {
286     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "vfb->active is not true");
287     return;
288   }
289 
290   stats->total_frames = atomic_load_u64(&vfb->total_frames_received);
291   stats->dropped_frames = atomic_load_u64(&vfb->total_frames_dropped);
292   stats->drop_rate = (stats->total_frames > 0) ? (float)stats->dropped_frames / (float)stats->total_frames : 0.0f;
293   stats->avg_decode_time_ns = atomic_load_u64(&vfb->avg_decode_time_ns);
294   stats->avg_render_time_ns = atomic_load_u64(&vfb->avg_render_time_ns);
295 }






References video_frame_buffer_t::active,
    atomic_load_u64(), video_frame_buffer_t::avg_decode_time_ns,
    video_frame_buffer_t::avg_render_time_ns, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_INVALID_STATE, SET_ERRNO, stats,
    video_frame_buffer_t::total_frames_dropped, and
    video_frame_buffer_t::total_frames_received.


Referenced by stats_logger_thread(), and
    update_server_stats().
